---
title: C. install package
---

Clone the submission pipeline repository and pull the prebuilt container images
from GitHub Container Registry.

::: {.callout-important}
## Requires: GitHub access (step B)
`make pull` authenticates via the `read:packages` scope added in the previous
step. Complete [B. GitHub access](github-access.qmd) before continuing.
:::

## 1. Clone the repository

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows: use Git Bash
Open **Git Bash** (installed with Git for Windows in step A). All `make`
commands in this manual must be run in **Git Bash**, not PowerShell.
:::

```bash
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/imi-bigpicture/bp-submission-pipeline.git
cd bp-submission-pipeline
```

::: {.callout-note}
## What are submodules?
The pipeline bundles several companion components (web app, data model, and
submission CLI) as Git submodules — without `--recurse-submodules` those
folders arrive empty and the pipeline will not start.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
ls bigpicture-metadata-interface
```

You should see files listed inside the folder, not an empty directory.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## You are now inside `bp-submission-pipeline/`
All commands from here through step J are run from this directory. If you open
a new terminal, run `cd bp-submission-pipeline` before continuing.
:::

## 2. Pull the container images

```bash
make pull
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
docker image ls | grep ghcr.io/imi-bigpicture/bp-submission-pipeline
```

Expected: one or more `ghcr.io/imi-bigpicture/bp-submission-pipeline/...` images listed.
:::

## If something goes wrong

**`make pull` fails with "lacks the read:packages scope"**

Return to [B. GitHub access](github-access.qmd) and run
`gh auth refresh -s read:packages`, then try again.

**Clone fails with "repository not found" or "access denied"**

Your GitHub account has not been granted access to the private repository.
Request access via the [servicedesk](mailto:{{< var email.servicedesk >}}).

**`make pull` fails with "no such file or directory" on `/var/run/docker.sock`**

The Docker daemon is not running. Start it and retry:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows / macOS
Launch **Docker Desktop** and wait until the whale icon in the taskbar/menu bar
shows "Docker Desktop is running", then run `make pull` again.

## Linux
```bash
systemctl start docker
```
On older systems without systemd, use `service docker start` instead.
Then run `make pull` again.
:::

**Submodule folders are empty after cloning**

You cloned without `--recurse-submodules`. Delete the cloned folder and re-run
the full clone command above.
